annotations.note: Class is annotation only, never engine logic. importance_map / targets / liabilities are curated from the research log with real, NCBI-verified citations (PROJECT.md §6, §7).

modifiable_positions.label: C3 secondary hydroxyl
modifiable_positions.rationale: Classic modifiable handle; the site of the by-hand localized / self-inactivating design that is this project's ground-truth validation (PROJECT.md §12).

modifiable_positions.label: C14 tertiary hydroxyl
modifiable_positions.rationale: Tertiary alcohol on the steroid core; a secondary handle option.

importance_map.reason: The C14 beta-hydroxyl is essential for Na+/K+-ATPase binding (hydrogen-bonds within the pocket, e.g. Thr797); altering it strongly reduces inhibition.

citation.label: Katz A, et al. Selectivity of digitalis glycosides for isoforms of human Na,K-ATPase. J Biol Chem. 2010;285(25):19582-92.

importance_map.reason: The C17 six-membered alpha-pyranone lactone is the activity warhead: its carbonyl hydrogen-bonds within the pocket and the ring fills the hydrophobic funnel (Leu125/Ala323/Ile800). Modifying it is the intended-but-risky move in the by-hand soft-drug design.

citation.label: Laursen M, et al. Structures and characterization of digoxin- and bufalin-bound Na+,K+-ATPase compared with the ouabain-bound complex. Proc Natl Acad Sci USA. 2015;112(6):1755-60.

importance_map.reason: The C3 hydroxyl / sugar position is not essential for inhibition but strongly tunes binding affinity and isoform selectivity (the glycosylation handle) - a preferred site to modify without destroying activity.

citation.label: Katz A, et al. Selectivity of digitalis glycosides for isoforms of human Na,K-ATPase. J Biol Chem. 2010;285(25):19582-92.

targets.name: Na+/K+-ATPase (alpha-1 / alpha-2 / alpha-3 isoforms)
targets.role: Primary molecular target; the cardiac-glycoside binding site. Bufalin binds all three isoforms with similar affinity.

citation.label: Laursen M, et al. Proc Natl Acad Sci USA. 2015;112(6):1755-60.

targets.name: ABCB1 (P-glycoprotein)
targets.role: Bufalin reverses ABCB1-mediated multidrug resistance (e.g. to docetaxel).

citation.label: Zhang D, et al. Bufalin reverses ABCB1-mediated resistance to docetaxel in breast cancer. Heliyon. 2023;9(3):e13840.

targets.name: Apoptosis / mitochondrial pathways (Annexin A2, DRP1)
targets.role: Bufalin induces mitochondrial dysfunction and apoptosis beyond ATPase inhibition.

citation.label: Li Y, et al. Bufalin induces mitochondrial dysfunction and promotes apoptosis of glioma cells by regulating Annexin A2 and DRP1. Cancer Cell Int. 2021;21(1):424.

liabilities.detail: Cardiotoxicity and a narrow therapeutic window from on-target Na+/K+-ATPase inhibition in cardiac tissue - the primary barrier to systemic use.

citation.label: Botelho AFM, et al. A review of cardiac glycosides: structure, toxicokinetics, clinical signs, diagnosis and antineoplastic potential. Toxicon. 2019;158:63-68.

liabilities.detail: Poor water solubility from the lipophilic steroid scaffold, limiting formulation and delivery.

citation.label: Cheng CS, et al. New therapeutic aspects of steroidal cardiac glycosides: the anticancer properties of Huachansu and Bufalin. Cancer Cell Int. 2019;19:92.

liabilities.detail: Rapid CYP3A4-mediated metabolism gives a very short half-life and poor systemic exposure.

citation.label: Soumoy L, et al. Bufalin for an innovative therapeutic approach against cancer. Pharmacol Res. 2022;184:106442.

liabilities.detail: Little intrinsic selectivity among Na+/K+-ATPase isoforms (binds alpha-1/2/3 similarly); the C3 sugar governs isoform selectivity.

citation.label: Katz A, et al. Selectivity of digitalis glycosides for isoforms of human Na,K-ATPase. J Biol Chem. 2010;285(25):19582-92.
